[PDF_PAGE 111 | score=21] COMBAT INITIATIVE During turn; characters' phases are performed in cer- tain order; The process of determining the order which phas- es are executed called Initiative. At the beginning of each turn; every character rolls 2010 and adds his RFL Attribute to the result: The total is his Initiative score . Note that this roll is open ended Iike an Action Check; any 10s rolled are rolled again ad added to the Initiative total; Characters take their phases in Initiative order; starting with the character who has the highest Initiative score the event 0i the character with the higher RFL Attribute acts first. Further ties are resolved y an additional tie-breaking die roll; character can choose delay part of his phase atter using part 0i It. For example_ character can Simple Action t0 run into position and then declare that he will use nis second Simple Action make an attack against target of opportunity: the stated conditions do not come up at all in the turn, the character'$ delayed phase moves on into the following turn: does not roll Initiative but continues to delay from the previ- ous turn; phase can be held indefinitely in this way: At the end of any turn; character who is delaying can also choose to stop delaying and roll Initiative normally in the next turn_ When the delaying character takes his phase (or the part of his phase that was delayed), all his Action Checks for that phase have TN modifier of +2 because the character must react using snap judgment: Note that it is impossible for single character take more than one phase in single turn. Combat Sense: Characters with the Combat Sense Trait reduce the delaying modifier to +1_ Combat Paralysis: Characters with the Combat Paralysis Trait increase the delaying modifier to +3. Initiative Modifiers The following skill
[PDF_PAGE 124 | score=12] COMBAT Knockout Check; bleeds every turn] Deadly: Leg severed [WV 12; Knockout Check at +6 TN; Lost Limb (3) Trait; bleeds every turn] HEALING Injured characters will naturally want t0 recover so they can go out ad get into the line of fire again (to the eternal dismay of their physicians). This section describes the process involved treating wounds and what happens to characters their wounds are improperly or incompletely treated Deadly: Arm severed (WV 10; Knockout Check at +6 TN: Lost Limb (31 Trait: bleeds every turn] Chest Minor: Broken ribs [WV 2; +1 to all TNs] Serlous: Minor organ damage [WV 4; Knockout Check; +1 to all TNs] Crltical: Major organ damage ad internal bleeding (WV 8; Knockout Check at +2 TN; +2 t0 all TNs; bleeds every minute] Deadly: Chest cavity crushed heart and lungs destroyed [character instantly killed] FAILED TREATMENT character fumbles any treatment roll (i.e. , rolls 2 on the Action Check dice), the target's condition actually worsens: the case of wounds the wound value of the target wound i5 increased by 1; if he is trying t0 stabilize dying character, the character dies immediately: Foot Minor: Metatarsal bones broken (WV 2; half movement: +1 TN to all RFL-linked Skill Checks] Serlous: Foot crushed [WV 3; half movement] Critical: Foot nearly severed (WV 6; Knockout Check; half movement; +2 TN to all RFL-Iinked Skill Checks; bleeds every minuteE Deadly: Foot destroyed [WV 8; Knockout Check at +5 TN; Lost Limb (2) Trait; bleeds every turn] UNCONSCIOUSNESS Another character can attempt t0 wake up knocked-out character by spending Complex Action. The player of the unconscious character must make an Action Check using the same TN as the Knockout Check (p. 1161, with success indi- cating he will wake up at the beginning of the following turn t
